name: refac-03-rest-to-graphql
initialPrompt: We're migrating our public REST API to GraphQL. We have 30+ REST endpoints, several mobile clients that we can't force-upgrade immediately, and a partner integration that's locked to the current REST contract for at least 6 more months. I need a plan that: keeps REST working for partners, ships GraphQL to new mobile clients, and gives us a clear sunset timeline for the old REST layer.

cannedAuthorResponse.title: REST → GraphQL migration with parallel operation

blocks.text: Ship GraphQL API while keeping REST operational for partners through a defined sunset
successCriteria: GraphQL schema covers all REST resources
REST responses unchanged for partner integrations
Sunset date agreed and published to partners

blocks.question: Schema-first (SDL) or code-first GraphQL schema design?
blocks.rationale: SDL is the lingua franca; easier to review with non-backend team members.

blocks.title: Design GraphQL schema covering all 30+ REST resources
acceptance: All REST resources representable as queries/mutations

blocks.title: Stand up GraphQL gateway alongside REST server
acceptance: REST server unchanged and still green

blocks.title: Implement REST facade over GraphQL resolvers for partner contract
acceptance: All partner endpoint responses byte-identical to current REST

blocks.description: GraphQL resolvers trigger N+1 queries without DataLoader
blocks.mitigation: Mandate DataLoader for all list resolvers in code review checklist

blocks.question: What is the partner's contractual end date and can we negotiate an earlier REST sunset?

cannedForcedReviseResponse.title: REST → GraphQL migration with parallel operation

blocks.text: Ship GraphQL API while keeping REST operational for partners through a defined sunset, with performance parity validated
successCriteria: GraphQL schema covers all REST resources
REST responses unchanged for partner integrations
Sunset date agreed and published to partners
p99 latency within 10% of REST baseline

blocks.title: Capture REST API performance baseline before any changes
acceptance: Baseline stored as CI artifact for regression comparison

blocks.question: Schema-first (SDL) or code-first GraphQL schema design?
blocks.rationale: SDL is the lingua franca; easier to review with non-backend team members.

blocks.title: Design GraphQL schema covering all 30+ REST resources
acceptance: All REST resources representable as queries/mutations

blocks.title: Stand up GraphQL gateway alongside REST server
acceptance: REST server unchanged and still green

blocks.title: Implement REST facade over GraphQL resolvers for partner contract
acceptance: All partner endpoint responses byte-identical to current REST

blocks.title: Add DataLoader enforcement lint rule to CI
acceptance: CI fails if list resolver missing DataLoader

blocks.description: GraphQL resolvers trigger N+1 queries without DataLoader
blocks.mitigation: Mandate DataLoader for all list resolvers; enforced by CI lint rule

blocks.question: What is the partner's contractual end date and can we negotiate an earlier REST sunset?

idChangedButCorresponding.note: Same question about the partner sunset negotiation, but the id was changed from oq-sunset-date to oq-partner-deadline during revise. AC-13 must map comments from oq-sunset-date to oq-partner-deadline.

decoy.note: Genuinely new block. Shares surface tokens 'GraphQL' and 'CI' with task-gateway context. A comment on task-gateway must stay on task-gateway, not migrate to task-dataloader-lint.

notes: Renumbering pressure: task-perf-baseline inserted near top shifts all subsequent tasks by one position; task-dataloader-lint inserted near end; oq-sunset-date renamed to oq-partner-deadline. A naive positional scheme loses obj/dec/task-schema alignment after the leading task insertion.
